Fresh Gwinnett market numbers, a five-minute drainage check, and a Saturday farmers market.
Gwinnett & Beyond
A short weekly update for homeowners, friends, and neighbors across Gwinnett and the surrounding counties.
Wednesday, July 15, 2026

Hey friends,

We are right in the middle of a Georgia summer, when a sunny afternoon can turn into a heavy downpour before dinner.

This week I have one useful market number, a quick check that can help protect your home after a storm, and a local Saturday outing.

Nothing complicated — just a few things worth knowing and sharing.
MARKET SNAPSHOT
Gwinnett buyers have more choices

Realtor.com reported 3,408 active listings in Gwinnett County during May 2026. That was about 11.2% more than May of last year.

More inventory gives buyers a little more room to compare condition, location, and price instead of feeling pressure to jump at the first home they see.

For sellers, the message is simple: competition matters. A clean, well-prepared home with a sensible price is more likely to earn attention.

Pre-send check: Source: Realtor.com Economic Research county inventory data for May 2026, updated June 3, 2026.
HOMEOWNER TIP OF THE WEEK
Watch where the rainwater goes.

After the next good rain, take five minutes to walk around the outside of your home:

  • Are the gutters overflowing?
  • Do downspouts carry water several feet away from the foundation?
  • Is water collecting beside the house or across a walkway?
  • Are leaves or debris blocking a drain?

A small drainage adjustment can sometimes prevent a much larger moisture problem. If water keeps collecting near the home, ask a qualified professional to evaluate it.
AROUND GWINNETT: LOCAL EVENT OF THE WEEK

If you are looking for something local this weekend, here is one Gwinnett option worth checking out.

Suwanee Farmers Market

The Suwanee Farmers Market brings local growers and makers together at Town Center. It is an easy Saturday-morning stop for seasonal produce, locally made products, and a walk around the park.

Tip: Check the City of Suwanee’s official events page for the current date, hours, vendor information, and weather updates before heading out.
CARTOON CORNER
“Technically, the water is going somewhere.”

The foundation may have preferred a slightly better plan.
